page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the ARMv8 architecture. It features 8 dedicated physical cores running at 3.0 GHz on an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor, with 1.0 thread per core and no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 32 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. There is no local NVMe storage bundled with this instance, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the t2a-standard-8 demonstrates average performance across most general-purpose workloads. Multi-core performance is solid, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 14472.4 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 6000.0. Memory performance is also average, delivering a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 563.91 GB/sec and a write large block uncached bandwidth of 47.24 GB/sec. The instance handles static web serving efficiently, achieving a throughput of 10.09 GB/sec, and performs well in database operations with a PassMark database score of 5611.0 and Redis SET operations reaching 10.91 million operations per second.

However, the ARM-based architecture presents certain performance tradeoffs. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 1073.0 and a weak single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 8.58 GB/sec. Cryptographic and vector-heavy tasks are also limited; the processor lacks AVX, AMX, and SHA extensions, resulting in a weak PassMark CPU encryption score of 1300.22. Additionally, LLM inference performance is poor, with text generation on a small 135M model yielding only 32.29 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t2a-standard-8 offers a highly competitive hardware profile for workloads that benefit from dedicated physical cores and balanced memory distribution without requiring high single-thread performance or specialized instruction sets. This makes the instance well-suited for scale-out workloads, static web serving, multi-threaded application compiling, and standard database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-8 is an ARM-based general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor and 32 GB of RAM. Lacking hyperthreading, local storage, and GPU accelerators, it provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. Benchmarks indicate average multi-core and memory performance, with strong results in static web serving at 10.09 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 10.91 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is weak, and the lack of AVX, AMX, and SHA extensions limits cryptographic and vector-heavy workloads. LLM inference speeds are also poor. This instance is best suited for scale-out general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, and database operations where dedicated core allocation is required without the need for specialized x86 instruction sets.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-8 is an ARM-based instance with 8 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores and 32 GB RAM, offering balanced multi-core performance for web serving and databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-8 is an ARM-based instance featuring 8 dedicated cores and 32 GB RAM, optimized for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated ARM-based instance delivering balanced memory and multi-core performance for scale-out workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 physical cores running at 3.0 GHz
32 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
Solid multi-core performance for static web serving and Redis operations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Weak single-core performance and lack of AVX/AMX instruction sets
Best suited for scale-out applications, web servers, and database workloads
